title: Nardurus tenellus
taxonomy: Nardurus tenellus is a member of the family Asparagaceae, a group of flowering plants that also includes asparagus and lilies. It is classified within the genus Nardurus, which is a small group of plants native to the Mediterranean region. The species N. tenellus is further divided into several subspecies, each with distinct characteristics. The classification of N. tenellus is based on its morphology, anatomy, and molecular characteristics. The taxonomic classification of N. tenellus has been revised over time, with some sources grouping it with other genera.

intro: Nardurus tenellus, also known as Sambucus ebulus, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and Western Asia, belonging to the family Adoxaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which contribute to its overall health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Nardurus tenellus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant are infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
